query: US Iran conflict Strait of Hormuz June 2026 MOU reopening agreement shipping
answer: The research report is complete with comprehensive structured data covering the US-Iran conflict, the Strait of Hormuz closure, the pending MOU/agreement, shipping disruptions, economic impacts, and prediction market assessments as of June 15-16, 2026.

key_facts: The 2026 Iran War began February 28 with Operation Midnight Hammer — coordinated US-Israeli strikes destroying Iran's nuclear facilities and killing Supreme Leader Khamenei
The Strait of Hormuz has been effectively closed for ~108 days (since Feb 28, 2026), stranding ~2,000 ships and ~20,000 mariners from 87 countries
On June 14, 2026, Trump announced the deal is 'complete' and authorized removal of the US naval blockade; Iran's Deputy FM Gharibabadi confirmed from the Iranian side
A signing ceremony is scheduled for June 19, 2026 in Geneva, Switzerland — VP JD Vance expected to attend for the US
Key deal terms: Strait of Hormuz to reopen immediately without tolls; full shipping restoration within ~30 days; ceasefire extension. Nuclear/sanctions terms undisclosed
The IRGC has NOT publicly endorsed the deal despite being the entity that operationally controls the Strait closure — widely cited as the single largest risk to actual reopening
Pakistan served as primary mediator (PM Sharif); Qatar joined as co-mediator from May 22
Brent crude peaked at $126.10/bbl in late April (from ~$72.50 pre-war), declining to $83.63 on June 15 on deal optimism
US gasoline surged 49% from $2.98/gal to $4.45/gal peak during the crisis
Metaculus gives 75% probability of deal signing before June 26, but only 40% probability of Strait actually reopening before June 27
Polymarket gives ~81.5% probability of US-Iran signing by July 31
Fitch assessed broad Hormuz reopening as unlikely before July 2026; insurance companies remain unwilling to cover Hormuz transit
Israel opposes the deal — Netanyahu struck Beirut on June 14 despite Trump's warnings; Israeli Security Cabinet may formally oppose
52 US senators and 177 representatives signed a letter opposing any deal allowing continued Iranian enrichment
Mojtaba Khamenei (son of deceased leader) was selected as successor Supreme Leader on March 8-9; his authority is not consolidated
The Islamabad Talks of April 11-12 (first direct US-Iran talks since 1979) lasted 21 hours but collapsed without agreement
Global oil supply loss estimated at ~2 million bpd (IEA); Iran loses ~$435M/day from the US naval blockade
S&P Global described the Strait closure as 'the biggest disruption in the history of modern energy'
